s-1.ptxt: INSTALL DIFFERENTIAL GEAR LUBE APPLY TUBE

s2.ptxt: Install the differential gear lube apply tube and transaxle apply tube clamp to the transaxle housing with the bolt.

atten3.ptxt: Make sure to insert the pipe to the stopper.

s2.ptxt: Install 2 new O-rings to the 2 No. 1 transaxle case plugs.
Install the 2 No. 1 transaxle case plugs to the transaxle rear housing.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL UNDERDRIVE OUTPUT SHAFT OIL SEAL RING

s2.ptxt: Coat a new oil seal ring with ATF and install it to the transaxle housing.
Coat the underdrive cylindrical roller bearing with ATF.
Using SST and a press, install the underdrive cylindrical roller bearing.

atten3.ptxt: Do not apply excessive pressure to it.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL UNDERDRIVE CLUTCH DRUM OIL SEAL RING

s2.ptxt: Coat 2 new oil seal rings with ATF, and install them to the transaxle rear cover.

item.ptxt: Do not expand the gap of the oil seal ring excessively.
Securely engage the hooks. Confirm the smooth rotation.

s2.ptxt: Wind vinyl tape around SST at the place 4.0 mm (0.157 in.) above the bottom end until the thickness of the wound tape is about 5.0 mm (0.197 in.).

atten3.ptxt: Clean SST to remove deposited oil before winding vinyl tape.

s2.ptxt: Coat a new needle roller bearing with ATF.
Using SST and a press, install the needle roller bearing to the transaxle case.

atten3.ptxt: When the wound vinyl tape contacts the transaxle case, stop press-fitting.

s2.ptxt: Coat 2 new O-rings with ATF, and install them to the underdrive brake piston.

item.ptxt: Install the the O-rings carefully not to have a twist or a pinching.
Apply enough ATF to the O-rings prior to installation.

s2.ptxt: Coat the underdrive brake piston with ATF.
Install the underdrive brake piston to the transaxle case.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the O-rings.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL UNDERDRIVE BRAKE RETURN SPRING SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Place SST on the return spring and compress the return spring with a press.
Using a snap ring expander, install the snap ring to the transaxle case.

item.ptxt: Stop the press when the spring seat is lowered to the place 1 to 2 mm (0.039 to 0.078 in.) from the snap ring groove to prevent the spring seat from being deformed.
Do not expand the snap ring excessively.
After installing the spring sub-assembly, check all of the springs are fitted in the piston correctly.
The snap ring should be securely engaged in the groove of the transaxle case.

s2.ptxt: Install the snap ring to the transaxle case.
Using SST and a press, install the bearing outer race (rear side) to the transaxle case.

item.ptxt: Ensure that the snap ring is securely installed.
Do not apply excessive pressure to the bearing outer race.

s2.ptxt: Using SST and a press, install the bearing outer race (front side) to the transaxle case.

item.ptxt: Do not apply excessive pressure to the bearing outer race.
Install the bearing outer race with a press while holding the bearing outer race with SST (09502-24010, 09523-36010).

s2.ptxt: Install the 2 angular balls to the transaxle case.
Coat the counter drive gear with ATF.
Using SST and a press, install the tapered roller bearing inner race (front side) to the counter drive gear.

atten3.ptxt: Do not apply excessive pressure to the bearing inner race.

s2.ptxt: Using SST and a press, install the counter drive gear and bearing inner race (rear side) to the transaxle case.

atten3.ptxt: Do not apply excessive pressure to the counter drive gear and bearing inner race.

s2.ptxt: Coat 2 new O-rings with ATF.
Install the 2 O-rings to the 1st and reverse brake piston.

item.ptxt: Install the O-rings carefully not to have a twist or a pinching.
Apply enough ATF to the O-rings prior to installation.

s2.ptxt: Coat the 1st and reverse brake piston with ATF, and install it to the transaxle case.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the O-rings.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL 1ST AND REVERSE BRAKE RETURN SPRING SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the return spring.
Place SST on the return spring and compress the return spring with a press.
Using a snap ring expander, install the snap ring to the transaxle case.

item.ptxt: Stop the press when the spring seat is lowered to the place 1 to 2 mm (0.039 to 0.078 in.) from the snap ring groove to prevent the spring seat from being deformed.
Do not expand the snap ring excessively.
After installing the spring sub-assembly, check all of the springs are fitted in the piston correctly.
The snap ring should be securely engaged in the groove of the cylinder.

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, install the front planetary ring gear and snap ring to the brake hub.

atten3.ptxt: Confirm that the snap ring is engaged in the groove of the brake hub correctly.

s2.ptxt: Install the front planetary gear assembly to the brake hub.
Using SST and a press, press-fit the front planetary gear assembly.

item.ptxt: Do not apply excessive pressure to the front planetary gear assembly.

Press the inner race of the LH tapered roller bearing, counter gear, and front planetary gear assembly to the position where no pre-load is applied to one pair of tapered roller bearings (left and right).

s2.ptxt: Install a new washer as shown in the illustration.
Using SST, install the nut.

Using SST and a torque wrench, measure the turning torque of the bearing while rotating SST at 60 rpm. If the measured value is not within the specified range, gradually tighten the nut until the turning torque falls within the specified range.

specitem.ptxt: 0.19 to 0.4 N*m (1.9 to 4.1 kgf*cm, 1.7 to 3.5 in.*lbf)

atten4.ptxt: Use a torque wrench with a fulcrum length of 160 mm (6.3 in.) to measure the turning torque.

s2.ptxt: Using a chisel and hammer, stake the front lock washer.
Coat the 2 thrust bearings with ATF.
Install the input sun gear, 2 thrust needle roller bearings, 2 No. 2 thrust bearing races, and No. 3 thrust bearings to the transaxle case.

item.ptxt: Ensure that the parts are installed in the correct order and direction.

table.title: Thrust bearing and bearing race diameter: mm (in.)

s2.ptxt: Install the rear planetary gear assembly to the brake hub.
Using a screwdriver, install the snap ring.

atten3.ptxt: Confirm that the snap ring is engaged in the groove of the 1st and reverse brake hub correctly.

s2.ptxt: Coat the 5 discs with ATF.
Install the 5 plates and 5 discs.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful about the order of discs and plates.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT PACK CLEARANCE OF FIRST AND REVERSE BRAKE

s2.ptxt: Using vernier calipers, measure the distance between the disc surface and the contact surface of the 2nd brake cylinder and transaxle case (Dimension A).
Select an appropriate flange so that the pack clearance will be within the specified range.

atten4.ptxt: Piston stroke = Dimension A - Flange thickness

s2.ptxt: Install the flange.
Coat the 2nd brake piston with ATF, and install it to the 2nd brake cylinder.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL 2ND BRAKE PISTON RETURN SPRING SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the 2nd brake piston return spring sub-assembly.

atten3.ptxt: Ensure that all springs are fitted in the piston correctly.

s2.ptxt: Place SST on the piston return spring, and compress it with a press.
Using a screwdriver, install the snap ring.

atten3.ptxt: Stop the press when the spring seat is lowered to the place 1 to 2 mm (0.039 to 0.078 in.) from the snap ring groove. This prevents the spring seat from being deformed.

s2.ptxt: Install the second brake piston assembly to the transaxle case.
Install the snap ring and measure the inside diameter.

item.ptxt: Make sure to install the tapered snap ring in the correct direction.
When the diameter does not meet the specified value, replace the snap ring with a new one.
After installing the snap ring, confirm that there is no clearance between the second brake cylinder and the fitting surface of the cylinder in the transaxle case.

s2.ptxt: Install the one-way clutch sleeve to the 2nd brake cylinder assembly.

atten3.ptxt: Check the positioning direction of the outer sleeve.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL NO. 1 PLANETARY CARRIER THRUST WASHER

s2.ptxt: Coat the No. 1 planetary carrier thrust washer with ATF, and install it onto the planetary sun gear assembly.

atten3.ptxt: After installing the washer, confirm that the projections on the washer are securely fitted in the holes of the planetary sun gear assembly.

s2.ptxt: Install the one-way clutch inner race to the one-way clutch.

item.ptxt: Check the direction of the inner race.
Confirm that the discrimination mark can be seen.

s2.ptxt: Check the rotating direction of the one-way clutch inner race for the lock or free operation, as shown in the illustration.
Install the one-way clutch and thrust needle roller bearing to the one-way clutch sleeve outer.

atten3.ptxt: Install the thrust bearing properly so that non-colored race will be visible.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR PLANETARY SUN GEAR ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Coat the No. 2 planetary carrier thrust washer with ATF, and install it onto the rear planetary sun gear.
Coat the bearing with yellow petrolatum, and install it onto the rear planetary sun gear.
Install the rear planetary sun gear assembly to the rear planetary gear.

atten3.ptxt: After installing the rear planetary sun gear assembly, confirm that the B1 discs engage.

s2.ptxt: Coat the 3 discs with ATF.
Install the 3 discs and 3 plates to the transaxle case.
Install the snap ring.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT PACK CLEARANCE OF SECOND BRAKE
INSTALL OVERDRIVE DIRECT CLUTCH HUB SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the direct clutch hub to the planetary gear assembly.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the bushing inside the overdrive clutch hub during installation.

s2.ptxt: Coat the thrust bearing with ATF.
Install the bearing race and the thrust bearing to the direct clutch hub.

atten3.ptxt: When installing the bearing, hold the side of the overdrive clutch hub.

s2.ptxt: Coat a new O-ring with ATF, and install it to the direct clutch drum.

atten3.ptxt: Ensure that the O-ring is not twisted or pinched.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL OVERDRIVE DIRECT CLUTCH DRUM SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Coat the direct clutch drum with ATF, and install it to the intermediate shaft.

item.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the O-ring.
Be careful not to damage the lip seal of the direct clutch drum.

s2.ptxt: Coat the overdrive direct clutch piston with ATF, and install it to the overdrive direct clutch drum sub-assembly.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the lip seal of the direct clutch piston.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL OVERDRIVE CLUTCH RETURN SPRING SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the overdrive clutch return spring sub-assembly to the overdrive direct clutch drum sub-assembly.

atten3.ptxt: When installing the spring sub-assembly, ensure that all springs are fitted in the piston correctly.

s2.ptxt: Coat the clutch balancer with ATF.
Install the clutch balancer to the overdrive direct clutch drum sub-assembly.

item.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the lip seal of the direct clutch balancer.
Ensure that the clutch balancer is not pinched and there are no other defects at the sealing lip.
Apply enough ATF to the sealing lip prior to installation.

s2.ptxt: Place SST on the clutch balancer and compress the piston return spring with a press.
Using a snap ring expander, install the snap ring to the direct clutch drum.

item.ptxt: Stop the press when the spring seat is lowered to the place 1 to 2 mm (0.039 to 0.078 in.) from the snap ring groove. This prevents the spring seat from being deformed.
Do not expand the snap ring excessively.

s2.ptxt: Position the end gap of the snap ring in the piston as shown in the illustration.

atten3.ptxt: Ensure that the end gap of the snap ring is not aligned with any of the stoppers.

s2.ptxt: Coat the 3 discs with ATF.
Install the 3 plates, 3 discs and flange to the intermediate shaft.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful about the order of the discs, plates and flange.

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, install the snap ring.

atten3.ptxt: The snap ring should be securely engaged in the groove of the drum.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL DIRECT MULTIPLE DISC CLUTCH DISC

s2.ptxt: Coat the 3 discs with ATF.
Install the cushion plate, 3 plates, 3 discs and flange to the intermediate shaft.

item.ptxt: Install the cushion plate with the mark on the white surface facing the plate.
Be careful about the order of discs, plates and flange.

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, install the snap ring.

atten3.ptxt: The snap ring should be securely engaged in the groove of the drum.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT PACK CLEARANCE OF DIRECT CLUTCH

s2.ptxt: Install the intermediate shaft and needle roller bearing on the transaxle rear cover.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the oil seal ring outers.

s2.ptxt: Using a dial indicator, measure the direct clutch pack clearance while applying and releasing compressed air (392 kPa, 4.0 kgf/cm2, 57 psi).
If the pack clearance is not as specified, inspect the discs, plates and flange.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT PACK CLEARANCE OF OVERDRIVE CLUTCH

s2.ptxt: Using a dial indicator, measure the overdrive clutch pack clearance while applying and releasing compressed air (392 kPa, 4.0 kgf/cm2, 57 psi).
If the pack clearance is not as specified, inspect the discs, plates and flange.
Coat the thrust bearing with ATF.
Install the direct clutch assembly and thrust bearing to the rear planetary sun gear assembly.

atten3.ptxt: The disc in the direct clutch should completely match with the hub attached outside the rear planetary sun gear. Otherwise, the rear cover cannot be installed.

s2.ptxt: Clean the contact surfaces of the transaxle case and the rear cover.
As shown in the illustration, place a straight edge on the direct clutch drum and measure the distance between the transaxle case and the straight edge using vernier calipers (Dimension C).
Using vernier calipers and a simple straight edge, measure the dimension shown in the illustration.
Calculate the end play value using the following formula. Select a thrust bearing which satisfies the specified end play value and install it.

atten3.ptxt: Make sure that the non-colored race side is facing the direct clutch assembly.

atten4.ptxt: End play = Dimension D - Dimension C

s2.ptxt: Install 3 new No. 1 governor apply gaskets to the transaxle case.
Install the clamp to the brake apply tube.

atten3.ptxt: Make sure to install the clamp to the apply tube before installing the apply tube to the transaxle case. This prevents the apply tube from being deformed or damaged.

s2.ptxt: Install the clutch apply tube.
Install the brake apply tube to the transaxle case with the bolt.

atten3.ptxt: Each pipe should be securely inserted until it reaches the stopper.

s2.ptxt: Using SST and a press, install the needle roller bearing to the transaxle rear cover.

item.ptxt: Face the inscribed mark side of the bearing race up.
Keep pressing until the specified value is obtained.

s2.ptxt: Coat the needle roller bearing with ATF.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR CLUTCH OIL SEAL RING OUTER

s2.ptxt: Coat 3 new rear clutch oil seal rings with ATF, and install them to the transaxle rear cover.

atten3.ptxt: The rear clutch oil seal rings should be securely engaged in the grooves of the drum.

s2.ptxt: Install 4 new O-rings to the 4 No. 1 transaxle case plugs.
Install the 4 No. 1 transaxle case plugs to the transaxle rear cover.
Remove any packing material and keep the contact surfaces of the transaxle rear cover and the transaxle case away from oil.
Apply FIPG to the cover.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Seal Packing 1281, Three Bond 1281 or Equivalent

atten3.ptxt: Apply FIPG in a continuous line (width 1.2 mm (0.047 in.)) along the sealing surface.

s2.ptxt: Apply liquid sealer to the threads of bolt A.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Adhesive 1344, Three Bond 1344 or Equivalent

s2.ptxt: Install the 11 bolts.
Coat the 3 discs with ATF.
Install the 3 discs, 3 plates and flange to the transaxle case.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful about the order of discs, plates and flange.

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, install the snap ring.

atten3.ptxt: The snap ring should be securely engaged in the groove of the drum.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT PACK CLEARANCE OF NO. 2 UNDERDRIVE CLUTCH

s2.ptxt: Using a dial indicator, measure the underdrive clutch pack clearance while applying and releasing compressed air (392 kPa, 4.0 kgf/cm2, 57 psi).
Select an appropriate flange from the table below so that the pack clearance will be within the specified range.
Temporarily remove the snap ring and attach the flange to the transaxle case.
Reinstall the snap ring to the transaxle case.
Install the underdrive clutch assembly to the one-way clutch.
Rotate the underdrive clutch assembly to check the rotating direction for the lock or free operation.
Remove the underdrive clutch assembly from the one-way clutch.
Install the outer race retainer to the one-way clutch.

atten3.ptxt: Securely install the outer race retainer onto the external tooth of the one-way clutch.

s2.ptxt: Install the one-way clutch to the transaxle case.

atten3.ptxt: Ensure that the outer race retainer and the mark on the transaxle case are aligned.

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, install the snap ring to the transaxle case.

atten3.ptxt: The snap ring should be securely engaged in the groove of the transaxle case.

s2.ptxt: Coat the bearing and bearing race with ATF, and install them onto the underdrive clutch.

table.title: Bearing and bearing race diameter: mm (in.)

s2.ptxt: Install the underdrive clutch assembly to the transaxle case.

atten3.ptxt: When installing the underdrive clutch drum sub-assembly, do not damage the oil seal ring.

s2.ptxt: Install the pawl pin and spring to the parking lock pawl.
Temporarily install the parking lock pawl, pin and spring to the transaxle case as shown in the illustration.
Install the underdrive planetary gear assembly to the transaxle case.

atten3.ptxt: Firmly engage all the discs of the underdrive clutch with the hub splines of the underdrive planetary gear assembly and securely assemble them.

s2.ptxt: Install the parking lock pawl shaft.
Install the pawl shaft clamp with the bolt.

Using a straight edge and vernier calipers as shown in the illustration, measure the gap between the top of the differential drive pinion in the underdrive planetary gear and contact surface of the transaxle case and housing (Dimension E).

atten3.ptxt: Note down dimension E because it is necessary for the following process.

s2.ptxt: As shown in the illustration, measure the 2 places of the transaxle housing, and calculate dimension F using the following formula.

atten3.ptxt: Note down dimension F because it is necessary for the following process.

s2.ptxt: Install the No. 1 thrust bearing race to the transaxle case while checking its direction.
Coat the thrust needle roller bearing and race with ATF, and install them onto the multiple disc clutch hub.

table.title: Thrust bearing and race diameter: mm (in.)

s2.ptxt: Coat the needle roller bearing with ATF.
Install the needle roller bearing to the multiple disc clutch hub.
Install the multiple disc clutch hub to the transaxle case.
Coat the thrust needle roller bearing with ATF.
Install the thrust needle roller bearing to the forward clutch.

atten3.ptxt: Install the thrust bearing properly so that the temper colored side of the race will be visible.

s2.ptxt: Install the forward clutch to the forward clutch assembly.

item.ptxt: Align the splines of all discs in the forward clutch with those of the multiple clutch hub to assemble them securely.
Be careful not to damage the bush inside the forward clutch hub during installation.

s2.ptxt: Install 2 new overdrive brake gaskets.
Install the differential gear assembly to the transaxle case.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL NO. 2 THRUST BEARING UNDERDRIVE RACE

s2.ptxt: Install the No. 2 thrust bearing underdrive race to the underdrive planetary gear assembly.
Coat the thrust needle roller bearing with ATF.

Calculate the end play value using the following formula and values of Dimensions E and F that ware measured when installing the cylindrical roller bearing and underdrive planetary gear. Select an appropriate underdrive planetary gear thrust bearing race No. 2 which satisfies the specified end play value, and install it.

atten4.ptxt: End play = Dimension F - Dimension E - Thrust bearing thickness 2.5 mm (0.0984 in.) - Underdrive thrust bearing race No. 2 thickness.

table.title: Bearing and bearing race diameter: mm (in.)

s2.ptxt: Install the oil pump to the transaxle case with the 7 bolts.
Coat the O-ring of the oil pump with ATF.

atten3.ptxt: Confirm the input shaft rotates smoothly with the manual operation after installing the oil pump.

s2.ptxt: Remove any parking material and keep the contact surfaces of the transaxle case and transaxle housing away from oil.
Apply FIPG to the transaxle case.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Seal Packing 1281, Three Bond 1281 or Equivalent

s2.ptxt: Install the transaxle housing to the transaxle case with the 16 bolts.

atten4.ptxt: Apply seal packing or equivalent to bolts A and C.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Seal Packing 1344, Three Bond 1344 or Equivalent

atten3.ptxt: Apply seal packing to new bolts and tighten them within 10 minutes of application.

s2.ptxt: Using a dial indicator, measure the input shaft end play.
Fix the transaxle assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL MANUAL VALVE LEVER SHAFT OIL SEAL

s2.ptxt: Coat a new oil seal with MP grease.
Using SST, install the oil seal to the transaxle case.
Install the parking lock rod to the manual valve lever.

atten4.ptxt: Align the dial with the notches on the manual valve lever and turn the dial 90° to install the parking lock rod.

s2.ptxt: Install a new spacer and the manual valve lever shaft to the transaxle case.

atten3.ptxt: Do not damage the oil seal while installing the shaft to the transaxle case.

s2.ptxt: Using a pin punch and hammer, drive in a new pin.
Turn the spacer and the lever shaft to align the smaller hole of the spacer with the staking position mark on the lever shaft.
Using a pin punch, stake the spacer through the small hole.
Check that the spacer does not turn.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL MANUAL VALVE LEVER SHAFT RETAINER SPRING

s2.ptxt: Using needle-nose pliers, install the retainer spring.

atten3.ptxt: Hang the spring on the shaft securely.

s2.ptxt: Install the parking lock pawl bracket with the 2 bolts.

atten3.ptxt: Be sure the parking rod is placed between the parking pawl and the guide of the parking bracket after the parking bracket is installed.

s2.ptxt: Install the manual detent spring sub-assembly with the 2 bolts.

atten3.ptxt: Make sure to install the manual detent spring and cover in this order.

atten4.ptxt: Tighten the bolts in the order of A and B.

s2.ptxt: Coat a new O-ring with ATF, and install it to the B-3 accumulator piston.

atten3.ptxt: Install the O-ring to the accumulator piston not to have a twist or a pinching. Apply enough ATF to the O-ring prior to installation. Be sure about the installation position.

s2.ptxt: Coat the piston and springs with ATF, and install them to the transaxle case.

atten3.ptxt: Install the correct springs to the B-3 accumulator piston by checking the color or dimension of the springs.

s2.ptxt: Coat 2 new O-rings with ATF, and install them to the reverse clutch accumulator piston.

atten3.ptxt: Install the O-rings to the accumulator piston not to have a twist or a pinching. Apply enough ATF to the O-rings prior to installation. Be sure about the installation positions.

s2.ptxt: Coat the piston and spring with ATF, and install them to the transaxle case.

atten3.ptxt: Install the correct spring to the reverse clutch accumulator piston by checking the color or dimension of the spring.

s2.ptxt: Coat a new O-ring with ATF, and install it to the C-3 accumulator piston.

atten3.ptxt: Install the O-ring to the accumulator piston not to have a twist or a pinching. Apply enough ATF to the O-ring prior to installation. Be sure about the installation position.

s2.ptxt: Coat the piston with ATF, and install it to the transaxle case.
Install the spring to the C-3 accumulator piston.

atten3.ptxt: Install the correct spring to the C-3 accumulator piston by checking the color or dimension of the spring.

s2.ptxt: Coat the check ball body with ATF.
Install the check ball body and spring.

atten3.ptxt: Regarding the installation of the spring for the check ball body, be sure to place the spring in the hole in the ball body. Be careful about the orientation of the parts.

s2.ptxt: Coat a new brake drum gasket with ATF, and install it to the transaxle case.

atten3.ptxt: Do not damage the lip portion of the brake drum gasket when inserting the gasket to the case. Apply enough ATF to the gasket prior to installation. Be careful about the orientation of the parts.

s2.ptxt: Coat a new transaxle case 2nd brake gasket with ATF, and install it to the transaxle case.

atten3.ptxt: Do not damage the lip portion of the transaxle case 2nd brake gasket when inserting the gasket to the case. Apply enough ATF to the gasket prior to installation. Be careful about the orientation of the parts.

s2.ptxt: Coat a new No. 1 governor apply gasket with ATF, and install it to the transaxle case.

atten3.ptxt: Do not damage the lip portion of the No. 1 governor apply gasket when inserting the gasket to the case. Apply enough ATF to the gasket prior to installation. Be careful about the orientation of the parts.

s2.ptxt: Check the manual valve lever position. Install the valve body to the transaxle case with the 17 bolts.

item.ptxt: Push the valve body against the accumulator piston spring and the check ball body to install it.
When installing the valve body to the transaxle case, do not hold the solenoids.
Temporarily tighten the bolts marked by (1) in the illustration first because they are positioning bolts.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL VALVE BODY OIL STRAINER ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Coat a new O-ring with ATF, and install it to the oil strainer.

atten3.ptxt: Install the O-ring carefully not to have a twist or a pinching. Apply enough ATF to the O-ring prior to installation.

s2.ptxt: Install the oil strainer to the valve body with the 3 bolts.

atten3.ptxt: Apply ATF to the bolts prior to installation.

s2.ptxt: Coat a new O-ring with ATF, and install it to the transmission wire.

atten3.ptxt: Install the O-ring not to have a twist or pinching. Apply enough ATF to the O-ring prior to installation.

s2.ptxt: Install the transmission wire retaining bolt.
Coat an O-ring of the ATF temperature sensor with ATF.
Install the ATF temperature sensor with the lock plate and bolt.
Connect the 7 solenoid connectors.

item.ptxt: Connect the connectors to A, B, C, D, E, F and G, starting with the shorter ones.
Apply ATF to the bolt prior to installation.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL AUTOMATIC TRANSAXLE OIL PAN SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the 2 magnets in the oil pan.
Apply seal packing or equivalent to the 18 bolts.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Seal Packing 1344, Three Bond 1344 or Equivalent

s2.ptxt: Install a new oil pan gasket and the oil pan to the transaxle case with the 18 bolts.

item.ptxt: Apply seal packing to new bolts and tighten them within 10 minutes of application.
Remove any oil or grease from the contact surface of the transaxle case and the oil pan with the gasket completely before installing the oil pan to the case.

s2.ptxt: Install a new gasket to the drain plug.
Install the drain plug.
Coat 4 new O-rings with ATF, and install them to the 4 No. 1 transaxle case plugs.
Install the 4 No. 1 transaxle case plugs to the transaxle case.
Apply liquid sealer to the "A" bolt threads.

specitem.ptxt: Toyota Genuine Adhesive 1344, Three Bond 1344 or Equivalent

s2.ptxt: Install the 2 sensors to the transaxle case with the 2 bolts.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL OIL COOLER TUBE UNION (OUTLET OIL COOLER UNION)

s2.ptxt: Coat a new O-ring with ATF, and install it to the union.
Install the union to the transaxle case.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL OIL COOLER TUBE UNION (INLET OIL COOLER UNION)

s2.ptxt: Coat a new O-ring with ATF, and install it to the union.
Install the union to the transaxle case.
Coat a new O-ring with ATF.
Install the hose and a new O-ring to the breather plug.
Install the No. 2 breather plug hose to the transaxle case.
Install the park/neutral position switch onto the manual valve lever shaft and temporarily install the 2 adjusting bolts.
Install a new nut stopper and nut.
Temporarily install the control shaft lever.
Turn the lever counterclockwise until it stops, and then turn it clockwise 2 notches.
Remove the control shaft lever.
Align the groove with the neutral basic line.
Hold the switch in position and tighten the 2 bolts.
Using a screwdriver, stake the nut with the nut stopper.
Install the control shaft lever, washer and nut.
